intent: Create a consumer-health report on 'sunscreen: what the science says and what people actually buy'. (1) Wikipedia — 'Sunscreen' and 'Ultraviolet': extract the mechanism of action, SPF scale definition, and known limitations. (2) One Stop Market — browse beauty-personal-care for at least 3 sunscreen products under $30 (name, price, rating, SPF if stated). (3) Reddit /f/LifeProTips — 2+ posts on how people actually reapply / mistakes to avoid. Report must: (a) ground biology via Wikipedia, (b) evaluate each product against that biology, (c) close with 'what Reddit gets wrong or right'. Cite every claim.
